skill.name: senior-architect
skill.description: Comprehensive software architecture skill for designing scalable, maintainable systems using ReactJS, NextJS, NodeJS, Express, React Native, Swift, Kotlin, Flutter, Postgres, GraphQL, Go, Python. Provides architecture patterns, system design workflows, and technical decision guidance for senior developers and architects.
skill.summary: Complete toolkit for senior software architects to design scalable systems with patterns, workflows, and best practices
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Security audit completed. Static findings are false positives: external_commands (33) triggered by markdown code blocks, filesystem access is legitimate output functionality, sensitive finding is standard .env setup. No actual security risks identified. Skill is safe for marketplace publication.

medium_findings.title: Markdown Code Blocks Misidentified as Shell Commands
medium_findings.description: Static scanner detected 33 'external_commands' findings in markdown files (SKILL.md, references/*.md). These are FALSE POSITIVES - the scanner misinterpreted markdown code fences containing backticks (`) as Ruby/shell backtick execution. No actual shell commands are executed by this skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Analysis confirms these are markdown code blocks with TypeScript examples, not shell execution. The backticks are used for code fencing in markdown.

medium_findings.title: Standard File Output Functionality
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'filesystem' access at scripts/*.py:107. This is legitimate functionality - the scripts write output to a file when users specify the --output flag. This is expected behavior for a reporting tool, not a security vulnerability.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: File write occurs only when user explicitly provides --output path. Output is JSON data, not sensitive system files.

medium_findings.title: Standard Environment Setup Flagged as Sensitive
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'Environment file access' at SKILL.md:128 showing 'cp .env.example .env'. This is FALSE POSITIVE - this is standard development practice for copying an example environment file during project setup. No secrets are accessed or exfiltrated.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Command copies .env.example to .env - standard setup practice. The .env.example file is meant to be copied and filled in by developers.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static scanner reported 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at multiple locations in markdown files. No evidence of cryptographic code or weak algorithms found in the codebase. This appears to be a scanner artifact or false positive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed markdown files - no cryptographic algorithms present. Scanner likely produced artifact.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static scanner reported 'System reconnaissance' at SKILL.md:85 and references. No evidence of system scanning or reconnaissance behavior found. Likely false positive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 85 contains reference documentation links, not reconnaissance code.

content.value_statement: Senior developers struggle to design systems that scale and remain maintainable over time. This skill provides architecture patterns, system design workflows, and technical decision frameworks to build enterprise-grade applications.
actual_capabilities: Apply proven architecture patterns (microservices, event-driven, layered) to new projects
Design system workflows for scalability, performance, and maintainability
Make informed technology stack decisions based on project requirements
Generate architecture diagrams and project structures automatically
Analyze dependencies and provide optimization recommendations
Implement security best practices in system design
limitations: Does not write application code - provides architecture guidance only
Scripts are templates that require customization for production use
Does not provision cloud infrastructure or manage deployments
Does not perform runtime security auditing

use_cases.title: Architect New SaaS Platform
use_cases.description: Design the architecture for a new multi-tenant SaaS application including frontend, backend, database, and infrastructure choices
use_cases.target_user: Technical leads and architects starting new projects

use_cases.title: Review Existing System Design
use_cases.description: Analyze an existing codebase and provide architecture improvements for scalability and maintainability

use_cases.title: Technology Stack Selection
use_cases.description: Evaluate and select appropriate technologies for a project based on requirements, team expertise, and long-term maintainability

prompt_templates.title: Basic Architecture Request
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to design a [type of application] that handles [number] users. What architecture patterns would you recommend? Consider [specific requirement].
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick architecture consultation for a new project

prompt_templates.title: Detailed System Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a system architecture for a [description of system]. Include: 1) Component diagram 2) Data flow 3) API design 4) Database schema 5) Security considerations. The system must handle [scale requirements].

prompt_templates.title: Technology Selection
prompt_templates.prompt: I am building a [application type] with these requirements: [list requirements]. Compare [Technology A] vs [Technology B] vs [Technology C] for the [component]. Recommend the best choice with rationale.

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the architecture of my existing [system description]. Identify: 1) Scalability bottlenecks 2) Security vulnerabilities 3) Maintainability issues 4) Performance concerns. Provide specific recommendations for improvement.

output_examples.input: Design an e-commerce platform that handles 100k daily orders with real-time inventory
output: Core Components: API Gateway, Order Service, Inventory Service, Payment Service, Notification Service
Database Strategy: PostgreSQL for transactional data, Redis for caching, Separate read replicas
Scalability: Horizontal scaling with Kubernetes, Auto-scaling based on CPU/memory
Security: JWT authentication, API rate limiting, Input validation, SQL injection prevention

output_examples.input: Compare React vs Vue vs Angular for a large enterprise dashboard application
output: 理由: Larger ecosystem, more hiring pool, flexible architecture, strong enterprise support
Considerations: Team learning curve, state management needs, long-term maintenance

best_practices: Start with requirements before choosing architecture - understand scale, complexity, and team capabilities first
Document architecture decisions with pros, cons, and trade-offs using Architecture Decision Records (ADRs)
Design for failure - plan for component failures and have graceful degradation strategies
anti_patterns: Over-engineering early - do not apply microservices patterns to simple applications that could work as monoliths
Ignoring non-functional requirements - performance, security, and scalability must be considered from the start
Technology hopping - avoid switching tech stacks frequently as it creates technical debt and slows delivery

faq.question: What programming languages and frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill covers React, NextJS, NodeJS, Express, React Native, Swift, Kotlin, Flutter, Go, Python, PostgreSQL, GraphQL, and more. It provides architecture guidance for any technology stack.

faq.question: Does this skill write code or just provide guidance?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on architecture design and provides guidance, patterns, and recommendations. The included scripts generate project structures and analyze dependencies, but detailed implementation code is outside scope.

faq.question: Can I use this for existing system refactoring?
faq.answer: Yes, you can describe your current architecture and the skill will analyze it for bottlenecks, security issues, and improvement opportunities.

faq.question: How do the included Python scripts work?
faq.answer: The scripts (architecture_diagram_generator.py, project_architect.py, dependency_analyzer.py) are starter tools. Run them with a project path to generate diagrams and analyze dependencies. Customize them for your specific needs.

faq.question: Is this suitable for beginners?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for senior developers and architects. Beginners should first understand fundamentals of software design, data structures, and web development before diving into architecture.

faq.question: How does this integrate with Claude Code?
faq.answer: This skill extends Claude Code with architecture expertise. You can ask architecture questions in natural language and receive detailed guidance, patterns, and best practices.
